Q5 Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Astronomer: Most stars are born in groups of thousands, each star in a group forming from the same parent cloud of gas. Each cloud has a unique homogeneous chemical composition. Therefore whenever two stars have the same chemical composition as each other, they must have originated from the same cloud of gas.
Which of the following, if true, would most strengthen the astronomer’s argument?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
It is given that each cloud has a unique homogeneous chemical composition and the stars originated from the same cloud of gas have the same chemical composition. Thus, it can be concluded that whenever a star forms, it inherits the chemical composition of its parent cloud of gas. Hence, option (c) is correct.

Q6 Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

People tend to estimate the likelihood of an event’s occurrence according to its salience; that is, according to how strongly and how often it comes to their attention. By placement and headlines, newspapers emphasize stories about local crime over stories about crime elsewhere and about many other major events.
It can be concluded on the basis of the statements above that, if they are true, which of the following is most probably also true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
We know that newspapers’ coverage of local crimes is stronger than that of non-local crimes.
Nothing is said about frequency of coverage so, we can not assume anything about it. However, all else being equal, we can assume that people tend to think the local crimes outnumber non-local crimes.

Q8 Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The difference between the compound interest and the simple interest earned on a sum of money at the end of 4 years is To find out the sum, which of the following information given in the statements P and Q is/are necessary?
P : Amount of simple interest accrued after 4 years.
Q : Rate of interest per annum.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
To find the sum, difference between C.I. and S.I., the time and the rate of interest are needed.
∴ Only Q is necessary.

Q23 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which one of the following oscillators is used for generation of frequencies for RF applications?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Phase-shift and Wein bridge oscillators fall under the category of audio frequency oscillators
used for generating low-frequency sinusoidal signals. LC oscillators fall under the category of
radio-frequency oscillators used for generating high frequency (eg. 10 kHz to 100 MHz). Thus,
LC oscillator is used for generation of frequencies for RF applications.

Not attempted

Consider the feedback system shown below.

 
 
The root locus plot corresponding to the above system is
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
A point on real axis will be a part of root locus, when there exists odd number of poles and zeros
left to that point. The root locus branch originate at pole and terminate at zero. Only option (b)
satisfies the above conditions.
